strengths: Demonstrated data cleaning and ETL expertise
Quantified impact on KPIs and process efficiency
weaknesses: No IMAX or TXEN system experience

improvement_bullets: Optimized data reconciliation workflows to support high-volume inquiries and claims-related datasets, ensuring 99% data integrity through automated validation checks.
Proactively anticipated and mitigated data quality issues by establishing automated monitoring pipelines, reducing manual data auditing time by 30%.
Applied rigorous data classification and standardization protocols while managing complex datasets, effectively bridging the gap between technical requirements and stakeholder needs.
Leveraged experience in database management and ETL processes to audit large-scale data environments, ensuring compliance with established enterprise system standards.
Collaborated with internal teams to resolve inconsistencies in cross-functional reporting, ensuring that high-volume data inputs were accurate, clean, and audit-ready.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Quality Analyst opportunity with Horizontal Talent. I am interested in this opportunity because of my commitment to data integrity and the opportunity to apply my technical background to ensure high-quality, actionable insights within a collaborative environment. What interests me about working for Horizontal Talent is its commitment to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture, which inspires me because I thrive in environments where diverse perspectives drive innovation and professional excellence. I believe my expertise in data validation and my proficiency in SQL and Python would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on precision and proactive root-cause analysis. In fact, at Shine Masters, I identified a bottleneck in our data processing, designed a new ETL pipeline to automate the intake of client data, and successfully reduced processing time by 40%. This experience directly aligns with the Data Quality Analyst need to standardize and classify data for effective analysis. Ultimately, I know that the Data Quality Analyst role at Horizontal Talent would allow me to bring my data cleaning and modeling skills to the core responsibility of maintaining data accuracy and completeness. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my cross-functional collaboration skills. I led efforts to clean and reconcile insurance testing data—often dealing with complex, high-volume inputs—which resulted in a 30% improvement in data accuracy. I am excited by the opportunity to use my ability to anticipate data issues proactively in working on data auditing and quality control at Horizontal Talent. I know that my technical proficiency and analytical rigor align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Quality Analyst role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
